The rain fell hard on the dense canopy above. Alex checked the map for the third time. "We need to move," Sam said, his voice trembling. The vines were thick and sharp. Priya spotted a faint trail in the mud. "Follow me," she commanded. Ken lagged behind, struggling with his pack. They had to leave the safety of the riverbank. The forest was unforgiving to the unprepared. They walked through the undergrowth. Night fell quickly. They huddled together for warmth. The next morning, the sun broke through the leaves. They saw a road in the distance. "We made it," Alex whispered. They stepped onto the pavement. The green wall receded behind them. They would never leave this place again. The air felt different now, lighter and cleaner. They looked back at the green wall of trees. they had found their way out, but the jungle would never leave them.
